name: Puaena Point
tagline: The North Shore's secret beginner break — mellow waves in Haleiwa Harbor while everyone else chases Pipeline.
parking: Small lot at the end of Haleiwa Road near the harbor; overflow along the road
bestTime: Summer months for the gentlest waves; winter still works on smaller swell days when the outer reefs filter the energy
talkStory: While tourists and pros crowd Sunset and Pipeline, local families bring their keiki to Puaena Point to learn on soft, knee-high waves that break over a sandy bottom inside Haleiwa Harbor. The point creates a natural shelter from the open ocean swell, so even when the North Shore is pumping double overhead, Puaena stays manageable. Haleiwa Surf Academy and other local instructors run lessons here daily. The vibe is pure local — you'll see more Pidgin spoken than English, more foam boards than performance shortboards, and more uncles pushing kids into waves than Instagram photographers. After your session, walk to Haleiwa town for acai bowls or shave ice. This is where North Shore kids learn to surf before graduating to the big breaks.
